title: Boat Rental in Coron: Costs and What to Know 2026
html:
Boat rental in Coron lets you reach the lagoons and wrecks of Palawan from the public pier at Coron town, and most private day boats sit in a band of roughly PHP 8,000 to PHP 35,000 in 2026, with shared island tours far cheaper. BoatHire24 lists verified vessels across Coron Bay and nearby Busuanga.

Where to set off in Coron

Most trips leave from the centre of Coron town on Busuanga Island, and a handful of resort jetties handle the larger boats. The protected waters of Coron Bay make for an easy start to the day before you reach the limestone scenery further out.

  • Coron Town Public Pier: the main departure point, a short walk from the market and the dive shops.
  • Coron Bay: sheltered anchorage where many bangka outriggers and motor cruisers load guests.
  • Coron Island: home to Kayangan Lake and Twin Lagoon, the classic first stop.
  • Busuanga Island: the larger island around the town, with resort piers near Concepcion.
  • Malcapuya Island: a white-sand beach reached on longer southern routes.
  • Banana Island and Bulog Dos: sandbar stops popular on full-day charters.

Which boat to rent in Coron?

For the lagoons and wrecks, a private motorboat or a traditional bangka outrigger is the practical choice, while a larger cruiser suits a full overnight trip. Coron is a place where the boat is the whole experience, so match the hull to your group size and the distance you want to cover.

  1. Bangka outrigger best for the classic Coron Island loop, seats up to 12 guests, from PHP 6,000 a day.
  2. Private motorboat ideal for a faster, smaller-group island tour, seats up to 8 guests, from PHP 12,000 a day.
  3. Speedboat good for covering distant beaches quickly, seats up to 10 guests, from PHP 18,000 a day.
  4. Crewed cruiser suited to relaxed full days with shade and a cabin, seats up to 12 guests, from PHP 30,000 a day; for a sense of the larger end you can compare the Maiora 26m on BoatHire24.
  5. Liveaboard expedition boat best for multi-day wreck diving, sleeps up to 16 guests, from PHP 60,000 a day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Coron?

Prices depend on the boat, the season and how far you travel, but the bands below give a realistic 2026 picture for the Coron area. You can compare verified boat listings to see exact day rates and what each price includes before you commit.

  • A shared island-hopping group tour typically costs from PHP 1,500 per person for the day.
  • A small private motorboat for a half-day starts around PHP 7,000.
  • A catamaran or larger cruiser for a full day runs from roughly PHP 30,000.
  • A crewed yacht with skipper and crew begins near PHP 45,000 per day in high season.

How does booking a boat in Coron work?

You choose a boat and date, confirm with the operator, and pay through the platform, with the local skipper handling navigation and the route. Coron is a guided-charter destination, so almost every trip includes a crew rather than a bareboat handover.

If you are new to chartering, it helps to read how boat rental works so you understand deposits, fuel, national-park fees and what counts as a full day. Coron Island levies separate environmental and terminal fees that are usually paid on the day, so ask the operator what is included.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Coron?

The dry season from December to May offers the calmest water and the clearest visibility for the lagoons and wreck sites. The wet months bring the southwest habagat wind and occasional storms, when some operators reduce trips to the open southern islands.

Local tip: aim for the morning slot to reach Kayangan Lake before the day-tour crowds arrive, as the climb and the viewpoint are quietest soon after sunrise, and the habagat wind tends to build through the afternoon.

A licensed skipper or crewed charter covers the operating requirement for visitors, so most renters never need their own certificate. Let the operator confirm the exact requirements for your route.

At a glance

  • Price band: from PHP 1,500 per person shared to PHP 60,000 a day private
  • Peak months: December to May
  • Typical trip length: full day, around 7 to 9 hours
  • Capacity: most day boats carry 8 to 16 guests
  • Currency: Philippine peso, PHP
  • Nearest hub: Coron town on Busuanga Island, Palawan

Questions about boat rental in Coron

How far is Coron Island from Coron town?

Coron Island lies only about 3 nautical miles south of Coron town, so the crossing to Kayangan Lake and Twin Lagoon usually takes 20 to 40 minutes depending on the boat and the sea state.

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in Coron?

Most visitors do not, because trips run with a local skipper and crew who hold the qualifications, which means you simply join as a guest. Confirm the arrangement with the operator when you book.

Can I dive the shipwrecks on a boat charter?

Yes, several private and liveaboard charters reach the World War Two wrecks in Coron Bay, where certified divers can explore Japanese vessels sunk in 1944 at depths suited to a range of experience levels.

Is island-hopping cheaper than a private charter?

Shared island-hopping tours are far cheaper per head, from around PHP 1,500 a person, while a private boat gives you control over the route, the pace and the stops for roughly PHP 7,000 upward per day.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Coron or list your own, and from the lagoons of Palawan to harbours across the globe the choice is yours; if you own a vessel in the region, you can rent out your boat to travellers looking for exactly this kind of day on the water.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Coron and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
